data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Adobe optimizes for user decision-making time by limiting choices presented at once.
data.technical_spec: Simplified design layouts and progressive disclosure.

data.title: Fitts' Law
data.description: Design principle used to enhance the accessibility of interactive elements.
data.technical_spec: Larger target areas for the most commonly used buttons.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Adobe considers cognitive load by organizing information into chunks.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Design consistency across products based on user familiarity with other applications.
data.technical_spec: Consistent use of color schemes and typography.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Elements are grouped together to improve usability and user comprehension.
data.technical_spec: Optimized spacing and alignment in UI design.
